text.title: Azure DevOps Boards Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Azure DevOps Boards methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Process template (Basic / Agile / Scrum / CMMI) MUST be chosen before any work items exist; switching after work items exist requires a documented migration plan.
rule.rationale: the template defines work-item types, states, and fields; post-hoc switches lose data and silently re-shape reports.

rule.statement: Area paths reflect ownership (team or component) and iteration paths reflect time (release/sprint); mixing them in a single dimension is forbidden.
rule.rationale: mixed area+iteration paths break the standard reports and stop the boards from filtering correctly.

rule.statement: Shared queries MUST be stored under a versioned folder (e.g. /Shared Queries/v2/active-bugs) with a date suffix; ad-hoc edits to shared queries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: shared-query drift silently changes every dashboard that consumes the query.

rule.statement: Every board column MUST map to exactly one work-item state; columns mapped to multiple states or empty mappings are rejected.
rule.rationale: mismatched columns cause cards to teleport between columns when the underlying state changes.

rule.statement: Swimlanes SHOULD represent classification (e.g. risk tier, customer segment), NOT additional workflow state; using swimlanes to model state is forbidden.
rule.rationale: swimlanes that encode state collide with column-to-state mapping and confuse the team.

rule.statement: Project Administrators group MUST be limited to <=3 people; team-leads use Contributor + scoped Stakeholder access for outside reviewers.
rule.rationale: broad Project Administrator membership is the #1 cause of accidental process-template overwrites.
